owasp_category: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
title: Replace Deserialization With Signed JSON
objective: Replace insecure PHP object deserialization of session_data with signed JSON.
briefing: Logs show a malicious serialized object was deserialized from the session_data cookie. The session handler needs to stop trusting raw serialized input entirely.
hints: Restarting the web server doesn't change how session.php parses the cookie.
The fix replaces unserialize() with a format that can be verified before it's trusted.
debrief: The patch replaces raw unserialize() with signed JSON: the session data is JSON (so there's no way to instantiate arbitrary classes from it) and it carries an HMAC signature that the server verifies before trusting the contents - if the cookie was tampered with, the signature check fails and the session is rejected. This addresses Software and Data Integrity Failures (A08:2021) at the root: never deserialize or trust data whose integrity you haven't verified, whether that's a session cookie, a software update, or a CI/CD artifact.

logs: [INTEGRITY] malicious serialized object deserialized from session_data cookie - code execution

patch /var/www/app/session.php --use-sig.output:
patched /var/www/app/session.php
session_data is now HMAC-signed JSON... done

logs_append: [FIX] session_data now uses signed JSON instead of unserialize()
